display.purpose: Bricolage Grotesque, weight 800. Headings, section titles, and the big hero numbers (total market cap, the day's move). Fixed rem scale, never clamp() in the dense UI.

title.purpose: Bricolage Grotesque, weight 700. Panel/card titles and sub-section headings on the void.

body.purpose: Geist, weight 400. UI prose and labels; capped at 65-75ch, held at AA contrast (never low-contrast for elegance).

label.purpose: Geist, weight 500, tracked 0.08em. Ranks, captions, small overline labels.

numeric.purpose: Geist Mono with tabular-nums lining-nums, tracking -0.01em. Every figure a trader compares — price, %, volume, cap, rank — right-aligned in tables. The hero material of the system.

shadows.name: glass-ring
shadows.purpose: Hairline ring on glass panels and the active nav pill — depth by a lit edge, not a drop shadow.

shadows.name: focus-glow
shadows.purpose: Periwinkle focus ring (ring-3 + --glow-accent) on interactive surfaces. Focus is one of the only two lit things (state/focus).

shadows.name: row-edge
shadows.purpose: Lit left edge on the hovered market row, paired with a leftward accent wash. The row you're on glows without a heavy fill.

shadows.name: coin-halo
shadows.value: blur(7px) — a scaled, blurred copy of the logo image behind the icon
shadows.purpose: Soft colored halo drawn from a coin logo's own colors; lifts on hover. Owned by <CoinIcon>, never inlined.

shadows.name: chart-glow
shadows.purpose: Soft luminous halo on the primary/hero data curve only (never axes or grid), so data reads as lit against the void.

shadows.name: space-vignette
shadows.purpose: Vignette on the fixed <Space/> backdrop, pulling the eye inward toward the data.

shadows.name: overlay-float
shadows.purpose: The one place true drop shadows are allowed: floating overlays (dropdowns, dialogs, toasts) over arbitrary content. Never to separate in-flow panels.

motion.name: ease-out-quint
motion.purpose: Default ease-out for data motion (sparkline draw-in, number ticks). No bounce, no elastic.

motion.name: interaction
motion.purpose: State transitions on buttons, inputs, nav, tiles.

motion.name: market-row
motion.purpose: The luminous hover sweep on dense list rows.

motion.name: ambient-drift
motion.purpose: The blooms drift slowly — the app 'breathing'. Pauses under prefers-reduced-motion, data-motion=off, and hidden tabs.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Periwinkle fill with near-black Accent Ink text — the one interactive brand surface. Hover brightens; active nudges down 1px.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; height: 2rem; padding: 0 0.625rem; gap: 0.375rem; border: 1px solid transparent; border-radius: var(--radius-lg, 0.75rem); background: var(--primary); color: var(--primary-foreground); font-family: var(--font-geist-sans, system-ui, sans-serif); font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; white-space: nowrap; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.18s ease, transform 0.06s ease; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: color-mix(in oklch, var(--primary) 80%, transparent); } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: translateY(1px); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; border-color: var(--ring); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px oklch(0.74 0.15 268 / 0.5); }

components.name: Outline Button
components.description: Glass-tinted secondary action. Hairline border over a faint fill; hover lifts to the muted tone. Never carries the accent as a fill.
components.css: .ds-btn-outline { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; height: 2rem; padding: 0 0.625rem; gap: 0.375rem; border: 1px solid var(--input); border-radius: var(--radius-lg, 0.75rem); background: color-mix(in oklch, var(--input) 30%, transparent); color: var(--foreground); font-family: var(--font-geist-sans, system-ui, sans-serif); font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.18s ease; } .ds-btn-outline:hover { background: color-mix(in oklch, var(--input) 50%, transparent); } .ds-btn-outline:focus-visible { outline: none; border-color: var(--ring); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px oklch(0.74 0.15 268 / 0.5); }

components.name: Search Input
components.description: Glass-fill field with a hairline ring; focus shifts the ring to Periwinkle with a soft glow. Placeholder holds AA contrast, never a faint gray.
components.css: .ds-input-search { width: 100%; height: 2rem; padding: 0.25rem 0.625rem; border: 1px solid var(--input); border-radius: var(--radius-lg, 0.75rem); background: color-mix(in oklch, var(--input) 30%, transparent); color: var(--foreground); font-family: var(--font-geist-sans, system-ui, sans-serif); font-size: 0.875rem; outline: none; transition: border-color 0.18s ease, box-shadow 0.18s ease; } .ds-input-search::placeholder { color: var(--muted-foreground); } .ds-input-search:focus-visible { border-color: var(--ring); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px oklch(0.74 0.15 268 / 0.5); }

components.name: Glass Panel Card
components.description: The atmospheric surface: translucent glass over the living space with a 16px backdrop blur and a hairline ring. Never a flat opaque card, never nested.
components.css: .ds-card { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 1rem; padding: 1rem; border-radius: 1rem; background: var(--glass); border: 1px solid var(--glass-border); backdrop-filter: blur(16px); -webkit-backdrop-filter: blur(16px); color: var(--card-foreground); overflow: hidden; } .ds-card-title { font-family: var(--font-display, 'Bricolage Grotesque', system-ui, sans-serif); font-weight: 700; font-size: 1.0625rem; letter-spacing: -0.01em; margin: 0; } .ds-card-num { font-family: var(--font-geist-mono, ui-monospace, monospace); font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums lining-nums; letter-spacing: -0.01em; font-size: 1.5rem; font-weight: 500; margin: 0; }

components.name: Nav Link (active)
components.description: Desktop nav item. Muted at rest, brightening to ink on hover; the active route is a glass pill with an inset ring.
components.html: MercadoPortfolio
components.css: .ds-nav { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.25rem; font-family: var(--font-geist-sans, system-ui, sans-serif); font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; } .ds-nav-link { padding: 0.375rem 0.75rem; border-radius: var(--radius-lg, 0.75rem); color: var(--muted-foreground); text-decoration: none; transition: color 0.18s ease, background 0.18s ease; } .ds-nav-link:hover { color: var(--foreground); } .ds-nav-link.is-active { color: var(--foreground); background: var(--glass-high); box-shadow: inset 0 0 0 1px var(--glass-border); } .ds-nav-link:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--ring); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Coin Icon (haloed)
components.description: The single source of truth for every asset logo. A round avatar over a soft colored halo drawn from the logo's own colors; the halo lifts on hover. Never inline the halo elsewhere — route every logo through this.
components.css: .ds-coin { position: relative; display: inline-flex; width: 40px; height: 40px; flex-shrink: 0; } .ds-coin-halo { position: absolute; inset: 0; border-radius: 9999px; background: conic-gradient(from 210deg, oklch(0.78 0.16 45), oklch(0.8 0.15 158), oklch(0.74 0.15 268), oklch(0.78 0.16 45)); filter: blur(7px); opacity: 0.5; transform: scale(1.12); transition: opacity 0.18s ease, transform 0.18s ease; pointer-events: none; } .ds-coin:hover .ds-coin-halo { opacity: 0.85; transform: scale(1.3); } .ds-coin-img { position: relative; z-index: 10; width: 100%; height: 100%; border-radius: 9999px; background: conic-gradient(from 210deg, oklch(0.78 0.16 45), oklch(0.8 0.15 158), oklch(0.74 0.15 268), oklch(0.78 0.16 45)); }

components.name: Market Row
components.description: The dense list's signature interaction: a faint accent wash sweeps in from the left plus a lit left edge, so the row under the cursor glows against the void without a heavy fill. Numerics are Geist Mono tabular, right-aligned.
components.css: .ds-row { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between; gap: 1rem; padding: 0.625rem 0.875rem; color: var(--foreground); transition: background 0.18s ease, box-shadow 0.18s ease; } .ds-row:hover { background: linear-gradient(90deg, oklch(0.74 0.15 268 / 0.08), transparent 42%); box-shadow: inset 2px 0 0 0 var(--primary); } .ds-row-name { font-family: var(--font-geist-sans, system-ui, sans-serif); font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; } .ds-row-num { font-family: var(--font-geist-mono, ui-monospace, monospace); font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums lining-nums; letter-spacing: -0.01em; font-size: 0.875rem; }

components.name: Direction Pill (gain / loss)
components.description: Market direction as a soft same-hue tint carrying gain/loss ink text. Never hue-alone: an explicit sign (+/−) is always present and an ▲/▼ arrow is added where color is the only other channel. Survives red-green CVD and grayscale.
components.css: .ds-pill-gain, .ds-pill-loss { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.25rem; padding: 0.125rem 0.375rem; border-radius: 9999px; font-family: var(--font-geist-mono, ui-monospace, monospace); font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums lining-nums; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 500; } .ds-pill-gain { background: oklch(0.8 0.15 158 / 0.14); color: var(--gain-ink); } .ds-pill-loss { background: oklch(0.7 0.17 20 / 0.14); color: var(--loss-ink); }

narrative.overview:
HODL reads the market as an atmosphere you look into, not a table you look at. The whole system sits on a deep blue-black void with real depth: slow-drifting blooms of light, a canvas starfield, a vignette pulling the eye inward. Panels are glass lifted off that space; the numbers — the actual data — are the brightest, crispest material on the screen, glowing faintly against the dark. It should feel like standing at an observatory window onto the market at night: calm, dimensional, quietly alive.

This system replaces the earlier "Trading Terminal" direction (flat graphite + acid lime), which read as the saturated AI-slop of every crypto dashboard. The move away from it is deliberate: the ambient light and depth are what make HODL not-generic, and any drift back toward a flat dark panel with a neon accent is a regression. It equally rejects the two crypto reflexes: the meme-coin casino (neon gradients, glow-for-hype, FOMO) and the navy-and-gold fintech template. HODL glows to convey state, never to excite.
keyCharacteristics: Deep-void, dark-native ground with a render-isolated ambient layer (blooms + starfield); light is a luminous "daytime sky" variant, not flat white.
Semantic ambient light — the bloom's hue carries the market's mood (green-teal when up, ember when down). The background reports; it never decorates.
One rationed luminous accent — Periwinkle — for interaction only, never on data.
Green/red held in reserve for market direction: luminous, but softened, never neon.
Glass panels + hairline rings + soft glow for depth. No flat opaque cards, no drop-shadow-as-elevation.
Monospace tabular numerics glowing faintly against the void are the hero material.

rules.name: The Three-Channel Rule
rules.body: Color carries exactly three meanings that never trade jobs: Periwinkle = "you can act on this," Gain = "market up," Loss = "market down." Periwinkle never colors a number; gain/loss never color a button or the ambient accent chrome.

rules.name: The Rationed-Glow Rule
rules.body: Glow means state or focus, nothing else. The accent glow (brand orb, focus ring) and the market glow (a rising number, a gain sparkline) are the only lit things; everything else is calm. If two glows compete, one is wrong.

rules.name: The Color-Plus-Sign Rule
rules.body: Market direction is never hue-alone. Every gain/loss value carries an explicit sign (+2.4% / −1.1%); the ▲/▼ arrow is added where color is the only other channel (heatmap tiles, ticker). Survives red-green CVD and grayscale.

rules.name: The Numbers-Are-Monospace Rule
rules.body: Every figure a trader compares — price, %, volume, cap, rank — is Geist Mono, tabular, decimal-aligned, from a single source of truth (the .tabular-nums hook). Nothing else uses the mono face.

rules.name: The Isolated-Space Rule
rules.body: The ambient layer (<Space/>) is fixed, memo-wrapped, and drives its own rAF loop — it must never re-render on data updates, and backdrop-blur on long live-updating lists must be applied to the container, not per-row, to stay smooth.

dos: Do set every figure in Geist Mono tabular, right-aligned in tables.
Do reserve Periwinkle for interaction only; keep it and every glow scarce (state/focus).
Do give gain/loss a non-color signal (sign always; arrow where color is the only other cue).
Do build depth with the ambient space, glass, and tonal layering — in that order.
Do keep the ambient layer render-isolated and honor prefers-reduced-motion + data-motion.
donts: Don't regress to a flat opaque dark panel with a neon accent — that is the rejected "Trading Terminal" / AI-slop look this system exists to escape.
Don't drift toward meme-coin casino (neon gradients, hype glow) or navy-and-gold fintech.
Don't color a number with Periwinkle, or a button/chrome with gain/loss.
Don't use background-clip:text gradient text, border-left/right >1px color stripes, or drop-shadow to separate in-flow panels.
Don't put backdrop-blur per-row on the live table, or let <Space/> re-render on data.
Don't use clamp() fluid headings in the dense UI, or proportional digits in a data column.
